title: rapuntium inundatum
side_effects: Rapuntium inundatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive consumption leading to nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. In severe cases, improper use of Rapuntium inundatum may lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can cause severe burns and blisters, requiring immediate medical attention.
